title: CanonicalEvent
description: The universal, immutable event envelope for the MEDRE pipeline. Every event is represented as a CanonicalEvent. Derived from msgspec.Struct with frozen=True.

event_id.description: Globally unique event identifier (UUIDv7 or similar time-sortable identifier).

event_kind.description: Event kind string from the kinds registry (e.g. 'message.text', 'telemetry.received').

schema_version.description: Schema version number for the payload structure. Must be >= 1.

timestamp.description: The moment the event occurred (UTC, timezone-aware ISO-8601).

source_adapter.description: Name of the adapter that produced the event.

source_transport_id.description: Identifier of the transport that carried the event (native actor/source identity).

source_channel_id.description: Channel/conversation ID at the source, if applicable.

parent_event_id.description: ID of the parent event in a derivation chain, if this event was derived from another.

lineage.description: Ordered list of event IDs forming the derivation ancestry.

relations.description: Typed links to other events (replies, reactions, edits, etc.).

payload.description: The event-specific data payload (validated per event_kind).

source_native_ref.description: Inbound native message reference carried from codec. None for outbound/internal events.

depth.description: Depth in the derivation tree (0 for source events).

NativeRef.title: NativeRef
NativeRef.description: Reference to a message in an adapter's native ID space.

adapter.description: Name of the adapter that owns the native namespace.

native_channel_id.description: Channel/conversation ID in the adapter's native format.

native_message_id.description: Message ID in the adapter's native format.

native_thread_id.description: Thread/parent message ID in the adapter's native format. Reserved — always None at runtime.

EventRelation.title: EventRelation
EventRelation.description: A typed link from one event to another (reply, reaction, edit, delete, thread).

target_event_id.description: The canonical event ID of the target event, if known.

target_native_ref.description: A native-space reference to the target, used when the canonical ID has not been resolved.

key.description: Optional discriminator (e.g. the emoji for a reaction relation).

fallback_text.description: Human-readable fallback text for the target when the event body is not available.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this relation.

EventMetadata.title: EventMetadata
EventMetadata.description: Top-level metadata container carried by every canonical event.

custom.description: Free-form dictionary for extensions and plugins.

TransportMetadata.title: TransportMetadata
TransportMetadata.description: Metadata describing the transport layer that carried the event.

gateway_id.description: Identifier of the gateway node that relayed the event.

delivery_confirmed.description: Whether the transport confirmed successful delivery.

transport_encrypted.description: Whether the transport layer provided encryption.

signature_valid.description: Whether a digital signature was verified.

propagation_state.description: Current propagation state as a transport-specific string.

RoutingMetadata.title: RoutingMetadata
RoutingMetadata.description: Metadata produced by the routing subsystem.

matched_routes.description: List of route identifiers that matched this event.

fanout_group.description: Name of the fanout group, if the event was broadcast.

route_trace.description: Ordered list of route IDs recorded per event delivery.

RadioMetadata.title: RadioMetadata
RadioMetadata.description: RF-layer metadata for radio-transport events (e.g. Meshtastic).

rssi.description: Received signal strength indicator in dBm.

channel_index.description: Radio channel index used for transmission.

metrics.description: Flat dictionary of metric names to numeric, string, or boolean values.

NativeMetadata.title: NativeMetadata
NativeMetadata.description: Opaque adapter-specific data that does not map to standard fields.

data.description: Arbitrary key-value data produced by the source adapter.
